tavily.raw: {"query":"COP30 Belém final text mandatory fossil fuel deforestation roadmaps exclusion Brazilian Presidency voluntary plans","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.theguardian.com/environment/2025/nov/22/roadmaps-adaptations-and-transitions-what-climate-measures-were-agreed-at-cop30","title":"Compromises, voluntary measures and no mention of fossil fuels: key points from Cop30 deal | Cop30 | The Guardian","content":"The Cop30 president, André Corrêa do Lago, said the plan to develop the roadmap to move away from fossil fuels had the support of the Brazilian president, Lula. # Compromises, voluntary measures and no mention of fossil fuels: key points from Cop30 deal. The UN climate summit Cop30 moved forward the fight against the climate crisis and the damage it is already causing to lives and livelihoods. But global heating requires a global response and in today’s fractured geopolitical climate sealing a deal backed by all countries keeps the UN climate show on the road. Fossil fuels were not mentioned in the key final decision, despite a coalition of 90 developed and developing nations having pushed for a commitment to a roadmap to phase out fossil fuels. With the roadmap for the transition away from fossil fuels was blocked from the formal Cop30 decision, the Brazilian presidency announced the plan would proceed outside the UN process.","score":0.66308063,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://healthpolicy-watch.news/cop30-ends-with-plans-to-make-more-plans-no-mention-of-fossil-fuel-phase-out","title":"COP30 Ends With No Text On Fossil Fuels Phase-Out - But Plans For A Conference In 2026 - Health Policy Watch","content":"The UN climate summit marking the tenth anniversary of the Paris Agreement to keep global warming under 1.5 °C ended in trademark UN fashion: a text laying out next steps to speak about plans to agree to make more plans. The outcome text included the first direct acknowledgement of the health benefits of mitigating emissions in a COP decision, while the Belém Health Action Plan – a voluntary policy package of best practices for adapting health systems to the climate crisis – was endorsed by about 10% of nations but received no money from governments. The action plan also invites nations to report progress on health adaptation in their submissions to the Global Stocktake at COP33, making health adaptation part of countries’ official climate progress reporting for the first time. In the closing days of the conference, more than 80 developed and developing countries, led by the United Kingdom and the European Union, had backed a COP commitment to developing a “fossil fuel roadmap” as well as a reference to “fossil fuel transition” in the outcome document.","score":0.6461259,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://earth.org/no-mention-of-planet-warming-fossil-fuels-in-cop30-agreement","title":"No Mention of Planet-Warming Fossil Fuels in COP30 ...","content":"A voluntary initiative by the Brazilian presidency, however, brings the world one step closer to the end of the fossil fuel era. Even after","score":0.6227582,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://oeco.org.br/english/roadmap-for-phasing-out-fossil-fuels-left-out-of-final-cop30-text","title":"Roadmap for Phasing Out Fossil Fuels Left Out of Final ...","content":"A voluntary initiative by the Brazilian presidency, however, brings the world one step closer to the end of the fossil fuel era. Even after negotiations","score":0.61057013,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://news.mongabay.com/2025/11/brazil-aims-for-alternative-route-to-fossil-fuel-road-map-after-cop30-failure","title":"Brazil aims for alternative route to fossil fuel road map after COP30 failure","content":"# Brazil aims for alternative route to fossil fuel road map after COP30 failure. * *Brazil will collaborate with the Colombian and Dutch delegations to develop the road map outside the formal U.N. process, with the goal of bringing it back for discussion at COP31.*. BELÉM, Brazil — In April 2026, a new summit in Santa Marta, Colombia, promises to advance on one crucial point the U.N. climate summits have been failing to address after 30 editions: planning the transition to a world without fossil fuels. Although Brazil spearheaded a road map to move away from planet-warming fossil fuels, it was never included in the official agenda — despite expectations following COP28’s call for a transition. As an alternative, Brazil, which will hold COP’s presidency until the next summit, offered to develop two road maps outside the formal process, one to transition away from fossil fuels and another to halt and reverse deforestation.","score":0.6072213,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.6,"request_id":"54659701-5839-4d1a-8021-93c93f39ef22"}
